title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Environmental Protection Agency relating to "Greenhouse Gas Emissions Standards for Heavy-Duty Vehicles-Phase 3".
plain_summary: This bill would cancel a new Environmental Protection Agency rule about pollution from big trucks and buses. The EPA rule was made to reduce harmful gases that come from heavy vehicles like garbage trucks, school buses, and big delivery trucks. The rule would have required these vehicles to produce less pollution over time. If Congress passes this bill, the EPA rule would be thrown out and would not become law. This means the pollution standards for these big vehicles would stay the same as they are now.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress has decided to reject a rule made by the Environmental Protection Agency. This rule was about greenhouse gas emissions standards for heavy-duty vehicles in Phase 3. The rule was published in the Federal Register on April twenty-second, two thousand twenty-four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will have no legal power and cannot be enforced.
